dtcs.description: or DTC indicator 49 : An Open in the Mode Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Clear the DTCs with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II). -3. Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -4. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121A or 49 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the mode control motor circuit.

DTC check: DTC check -1. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1233 or 40 and/or B2986 or 55 indicated? -YES- Repair an open in the wire between the climate control unit and the mode control motor. -NO- Go to step 3.

Open wire check (MODE-P line): Open wire check (MODE-P line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -5. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121A or 49 indicated? -YES- Repair an open in the wire between the climate control unit and the mode control motor. -NO- The MODE-P wire is OK. Replace the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Mode Control Motor Removal and Installation.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator 4A : A Short in the Mode Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Clear the DTCs with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II). -3. Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -4. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121B or 4A indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the mode control motor circuit.

DTC check: DTC check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -5. Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B121B or 4A indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Replace the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Mode Control Motor Removal and Installation.
Mode control motor S5V line check: Mode control motor S5V line check -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there 5 V? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 5.

Shorted wire check (MDD-P line): Shorted wire check (MDD-P line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the wire between the climate control unit and the mode control motor. -NO- The MDD-P wire is OK. Replace the climate control unit.

Shorted wire check (S_5V line): Shorted wire check (S_5V line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the wire between the climate control unit and the mode control motor. -NO- The S_5V wire is OK. Replace the climate control unit.

Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -4. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121D or 89 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure.

Humidity/in-car temperature sensor S_5V line check: Humidity/in-car temperature sensor S_5V line check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 5 V? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 7.

Open wire check (SENSOR_COM line): Open wire check (SENSOR_COM line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The SENSOR_COM wire is OK. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor.

Climate control unit HUM_DATA, TR lines check: Climate control unit HUM_DATA, TR lines check -1. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 8.
Climate control unit HUM_DATA line check: Climate control unit HUM_DATA line check -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 6. -NO- Go to step 9.

Shorted wire check (HUM_DATA line): Shorted wire check (HUM_DATA line) -1. Disconnect the jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short to body ground in the YEL wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor. -NO- Repair a short to body ground in the LT BLU wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor.

Open wire check (S_5V line): Open wire check (S_5V line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the wires between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor.

Open wire check (HUM_DATA line): Open wire check (HUM_DATA line) -1. Disconnect the jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair an open in the LT BLU wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor. -NO- Repair an open in the YEL wire between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor.

Shorted wire check (HUM_DATA, TR lines): Shorted wire check (HUM_DATA, TR lines) -1. Disconnect the jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the wires between the climate control unit and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor. -NO- Go to step 10.

Climate control unit check: Climate control unit check -1. Reconnect the climate control unit 32P connector. -2. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Press and hold the AUTO and ON/OFF buttons. Does the mode indicator turn on? -YES- Replace the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair. -NO- Replace the climate control unit.

steps.instruction: Problem verification (passenger's door lock switch in the neutral position): Problem verification (passenger's door lock switch in the neutral position) -1. Check the ON/OFF information of the FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Is either information indicator ON? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification (passenger's door lock switch in the lock position): Problem verification (passenger's door lock switch in the lock position) -1. With the front passenger's door lock switch in the lock position, check the ON/OFF information of the FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators ON? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

Shorted wire check (AS_REMOTE_SW_LOCK to AS_REMOTE_SW_UNLOCK line): Shorted wire check (AS_REMOTE_SW_LOCK to AS_REMOTE_SW_ UNLOCK line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -4. Check the ON/OFF information of the FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators ON? -YES- Repair a short between the wires. -NO- Faulty door lock switch; replace the front passenger's power window switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passengers Power Window Switch Removal and Installation (4-door).

Passenger's door lock switch check: Passenger's door lock switch check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check the ON/OFF information of the FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and FRONT PASSENGER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Is either information indicator ON? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Faulty door lock switch; replace the front passenger's power window switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passengers Power Window Switch Removal and Installation (4-door).

Shorted wire check (AS_REMOTE_SW_LOCK, AS_REMOTE_SW_UNLOCK lines): Shorted wire check (AS_REMOTE_SW_LOCK, AS_REMOTE_SW_ UNLOCK lines) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2 individually. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short to ground in the wire. -NO- Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Relay Box/Service and Repair.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1036 indicated? -YES- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the gauge control module (tach). -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

dtcs.description: Wiper Switch (As) Malfunction

steps.instruction: Determine possible failure area (windshield wiper motor and its drive circuit, FR_WIPER_AS line): Determine possible failure area (windshield wiper motor and its drive circuit, FR_WIPER_AS line) -1. Clear the DTCs with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II). -3. Turn the windshield wiper switch to LOW or HIGH for 15 seconds or more, then turn the switch OFF. Does the wiper motor work? -YES- FR_WIPER_AS line: go to step 2. -NO- Windshield wiper motor and its drive circuit: go to step 5.
Problem verification (DTC): Problem verification (DTC) -1. Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1077 indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

Windshield wiper motor check (AS signal): Windshield wiper motor check (AS signal) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Do the windshield wiper motor test. Does the wiper motor run and does it pulse normally? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair.

Open or shorted wire check (FR_WIPER_AS line): Open or shorted wire check (FR_WIPER_AS line) -1. Disconnect the following connectors. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short to ground in the wire. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Fuse check: Fuse check -1. Check the following fuse. Is the fuse OK? -YES- Go to step 6. -NO- Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair the short to ground on the No. B46 (30 A) fuse circuit.

Open wire check (IG1_FR_WIPER line): Open wire check (IG1_FR_WIPER line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Remove the windshield wiper intermittent relay. -3. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Windshield wiper intermittent relay and windshield wiper high/low relay check: Windshield wiper intermittent relay and windshield wiper high/low relay check -1. Do the power relay test  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Are both relay OK? -YES- Go to step 8. -NO- Replace faulty relay.

Open wire check (GND line): Open wire check (GND line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 9. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G401).

Open wire check (FR_WIPER_HI line): Open wire check (FR_WIPER_HI line) -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. Remove the windshield wiper high/low relay. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 10. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Open wire check (FR_WIPER_LO line): Open wire check (FR_WIPER_LO line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 11. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Windshield wiper motor check: Windshield wiper motor check -1. Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test. Does the wiper motor run and does it pulse normally? -YES- Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Relay Box/Service and Repair. -NO- Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II). -5. Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1079 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Fuse check: Fuse check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check the following fuses. Are the fuses OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. B38 (10 A) and/or No. B39 (10 A) fuse circuit(s).

MICU check: MICU check -1. Turn the headlight (high beam) ON. Do both headlights (high beam) come on? -YES- Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Relay Box/Service and Repair. -NO- Go to step 4.
Headlight bulb visual check: Headlight bulb visual check -1. Check the headlight (high beam) bulbs. Are the headlight (high beam) bulbs OK? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the faulty bulb.

Open wire check (L_H/L_HI line): Open wire check (L_H/L_HI line) -1. Disconnect the following connectors. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 6. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Open wire check (R_H/L_HI line): Open wire check (R_H/L_HI line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Open wire check (GND line): Open wire check (GND line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2 individually. Is there continuity? -YES- Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Relay Box/Service and Repair. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G201, G301).

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Check the fuel gauge. Does the gauge move to the empty position and the low fuel indicator blink? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Connect terminals A and B with a 2 Ohms resistor and a jumper wire. -4. Remove the No. A29 (10 A) fuse from the under-hood fuse/relay box for at least 10 seconds, then reinstall it. -5. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -6. Check the fuel gauge. Does the gauge move to the full position? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 3.

Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line): Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Replace the fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Removal and Installation. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator 59 : A Problem in the Blower Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II). -3. Do the self-diagnostic with the HDS or the climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Climate Control System. -4. Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1241 or 59 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the blower motor circuit.

Fuse check: Fuse check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check the following fuses. Are the fuses OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Replace the fuse(s), and recheck. If fuse(s) blow again, check for a short in the No. A2-8 (40 A) fuse and/or the No. B22 (7.5 A) fuse circuit.

Blower motor power circuit check: Blower motor power circuit check -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 10.

Blower motor operation check: Blower motor operation check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect the blower 2P connector. -3. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Does the blower motor run? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the blower motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Blower Motor/Service and Repair/Blower Motor Removal and Installation .

Open wire check (GND line): Open wire check (GND line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the jumper wire. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The GND wire is OK. Go to step 6. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the power transistor and body ground. If the wire is OK, check for poor ground at G401.

Open wire check (BLW-M line): Open wire check (BLW-M line) -1. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Does the blower motor run at high speed? -YES- The BLW-M wire is OK. Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the power transistor and the blower motor.

Shorted wire check (BLOWER_G, BLOWER_V lines): Shorted wire check (BLOWER_G, BLOWER_V lines) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the jumper wire. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2 individually. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit and the power transistor. -NO- The BLOWER_G and BLOWER_V wires are OK. Go to step 8.

Open wire check (BLOWER_G/BLW-G, BLOWER_V/BLW-V lines): Open wire check (BLOWER_G/BLW-G, BLOWER_V/BLW-V lines) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2 respectively. Is there continuity? -YES- The BLOWER_G/BLW-G and BLOWER_V/BLW-V wires are OK. Go to step 9. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the climate control unit and the power transistor.

Power transistor check: Power transistor check -1. Reconnect the climate control unit 32P connector. -2. Test the power transistor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Power Transistor HVAC/Testing and Inspection/Blower Power Transistor Test (Climate Control). Is the power transistor OK? -YES- Replace the climate control unit . See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair -NO- Replace the power transistor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Power Transistor HVAC/Service and Repair.

Blower motor relay check: Blower motor relay check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the blower motor relay from the under-hood fuse/relay box, and test it  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the relay OK? -YES- Go to step 11. -NO- Replace the blower motor relay.

Open wire check (+B_HTR_MTR line): Open wire check (+B_HTR_MTR line) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The +B_HTR MTR wire is OK. Go to step 12. -NO- Open in +B_HTR_MTR wire. Replace the under-hood fuse/relay box.

Open wire check (IG2_A/C line): Open wire check (IG2_A/C line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The IG2_A/C wire is OK. Go to step 13. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the No. B22 (7.5 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box and the blower motor relay.

Open wire check (GND line): Open wire check (GND line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair an open in the wire between the blower motor relay and the blower motor. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the blower motor relay and body ground. If the wire is OK, check for poor ground at G401.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST. -2. Has the data list value changed from OFF to ON? -YES- Replace the combination light switch  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair. -NO- Go to step 6.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Relay Box/Service and Repair. -NO- Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
